name: Cranberry-Apple Sorbet
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-size saucepan, combine the chopped apples and cranberry juice.
2. Heat to boiling.
3. Reduce heat to simmer, cover, and cook for 20 minutes or until the apples are very soft.
4. Uncover and set aside to cool to room temperature.
5. Puree the apple and juice mixture in a food processor or blender until smooth.
6. Pour into an ice-cream maker and process according to manufacturer's directions, OR
7. If not using an ice-cream maker, pour puree into a 9-inch square pan. Cover and freeze until partially frozen -- about 2 hours.
8. Meanwhile, chill a large bowl and beaters of an electric mixer.
9. Place the puree in the chilled bowl and beat on low speed until pieces are broken up, then beat on high speed until smooth and fluffy -- about 1 minute.
10. Pack sorbet into freezer container and freeze several hours before serving.
Nutritional Info: (per 1/2 cup) 2 g. protein, 27 g. carbohydrate, .3 g. fat, 2 g. fiber, 2 mg. sodium, 108 calories.
